articles.title: IT DOESN'T HAVE TO BE LONELY AT THE TOP
articles.summary: As a product leader, you're constantly making strategic decisions based on limited data. It's not easy — but you don't have to go at it alone.

articles.title: “AUTHENTIC” IS DEAD. AND SO IS “IS DEAD”
articles.summary: To stand out, avoid overused, meaningless phrases and words, and instead focus on being specific, showing rather than telling and embracing genuine, detailed descriptions that truly illustrate your value and impact.

articles.title: SELL-> DESIGN -> BUILD
articles.summary: This framework emphasizes pre-selling a product to gauge customer desperation and ensure product-market fit before incurring the high costs of development, contrasting with the common but less effective approach of building first and selling later.

articles.title: GOOGLE: AI SPENDING SPREE
articles.summary: Google is ramping up AI investments to fend off competitors, while OpenAI's SearchGPT aims to disrupt the search engine market. Despite its potential, OpenAI faces challenges in accuracy, misinformation, and user engagement. A clear path to profitability is needed as the company seeks additional funding.

articles.title: IS THIS THING ON?
articles.summary: Products should be designed to be essential and default On for users, ensuring immediate and widespread adoption, while overcoming the challenges associated with opt-in features.

articles.title: HOW TO UNLOCK THE POWER OF A CAB
articles.summary: PMs can tap into direct customer insights by establishing a Customer Advisory Board that offers in-depth feedback and a clearer understanding of customer needs. This involves maintaining consistency in CAB membership, setting specific goals, respecting member time, and ensuring actionable follow-ups post-meetings.

articles.title: 5 GTM PRINCIPLES YOU SHOULD KNOW AS A PM
articles.summary: Achieving product-market fit is a systematic process involving assumption validation in early go-to-market stages. Focus on a small, targeted segment for initial traction, ensuring early customers represent your ideal audience. Build growth loops that enhance user experience and provide real value.

articles.title: BUILDING TRUST AROUND CUSTOMER REQUESTS
articles.summary: Earn trust by deeply understanding the product and market, then leverage that trust to thoughtfully consider and respond to requests. Building credibility with sales and customer success teams is vital for effective collaboration and joint decision-making in a product's direction.

articles.title: HOW TO OPEN COMMUNICATION
articles.summary: Conversations are more productive when you seek to understand rather than solve. Asking questions like "Tell me more," "Could you share an example," "What led you to this decision," and "What does support look like to you?" helps reveal the full picture. This approach opens dialogue and fosters progress.

articles.title: SOFTWARE ESTIMATES SUCK? HERE'S WHAT TO DO
articles.summary: Humans tend to be over-optimistic about the near term and under-optimistic about the long term. Here are 5 alternative approaches to product roadmaps that focus on iterative problem-solving rather than traditional time and task-driven methods, helping to communicate progress more effectively to stakeholders.

articles.title: THE DIFFERENCE BETWEEN PM / HEAD OF / CPO ROLES
articles.summary: PM careers typically progress through four levels: Individual Contributor (working on cross-functional teams to ship features), Manager (managing and coaching PMs), Functional Lead (defining product strategy and processes), and Executive (overseeing multiple functions and addressing high-level strategic issues).

articles.title: WHAT ARE YOU MEASURING
articles.summary: Metrics are proxies and not the main goal.
